California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 14, 1990

Judy Davis
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-468

Dear Ms. Davis:

In August 1989, you asked for written confirmation of
telephone advice I had provided to you concerning Government Code
Section 84305.1/ I am writing to confirm that advice. I
apologize for the long delay in providing this letter. Apparently
I lost track of your request for confirmation and the fact that I
had not provided any written response to you.

You had inquired about compliance with sender identification
requirements for multiple-candidate controlled ballot measure
committees.2/ Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that
the requirements of Section 84305 would be met if all controlling
candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope, and, in
addition, on at least one of the inserts in the required type
size. Please note that if the mailing is sent by one committee,
the sender identification is required only on the outside of the

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ You did not provide the name of the client on whose behalf you
were seeking assistance. Accordingly, we consider your letter to
be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c).

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-89-468
Page 2

envelope.  (Section 84305(b).)  I also advised you that including
a list of the controlling candidates in the committee letterhead,
in the required type size, would be sufficient for the "inside"
sender identification, when such identification is necessary.

     Again, I apologize for any inconvenience resulting from my
delay in providing written confirmation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED:plh

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-0800

770 L STREET, SUITE 510
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

FILE NUMBER

6411.01

August 9, 1989


Ms. Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Confirmation of Oral Advice: Sender Identification
Where Sender is a Ballot Measure Committee
Controlled by Multiple Candidates

Dear Kathy:

This letter is to confirm the advice you rendered during our discussion on August 2, 1989. I inquired about the "sender identification" (Government Code section 84303) required where a ballot measure committee controlled by multiple candidates sends a mass mailing.

Based on the phrase "accompanied by" in Government Code section 68:84106, you advised that a multiple - candidate controlled ballot measure committee would be in compliance with Government Code section 84305 if, on its mass mailings, all controlling candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope, and, in addition, on at least one of the inserts (such as a page of a letter) in the required type size. Inclusion of candidates' names in the letterhead would satisfy the "insert" sender identification requirement. That requirement would also be satisfied if, where the controlling candidates were among the members of the measure committee's board of directors, the candidates' names appeared in the margin in a list of directors printed in the requisite type size. So long as all candidates were clearly identified as directors of the committee, no special emphasis need be given their names. You believed that all the candidates' names need not be "included" in the measure committee's name on mass mailings, so long as their names "accompanied" the Committee's in the manner described above.

Since we are advising clients based our understanding, as described above, I would appreciate written confirmation that our understanding is correct. Also, if we have misunderstood, could you please call me or Chuck Bell at your earliest possible convenience to inform us of any different interpretation. I realize that written confirmation or clarification may not be

Ms. Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
August 9, 1989
Page 2


delayed by the press of the Commission's other urgent business,
but we do need to know of any clarifications immediately so that
we can notify clients.

Thank you for your courtesy.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

JUDITH SPROUL DAVIS

JSD:ss

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 11, 1989


Judith Sproul Davis
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-468

Dear Ms. Davis:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on August 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you
as to the information needed. If your request is for informal
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh

428 I Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Dear Ms. Davis:

sections.question: You had inquired about compliance with sender identification requirements for multiple-candidate controlled ballot measure committees. Specifically, regarding whether a multiple-candidate controlled ballot measure committee would be in compliance with Government Code section 84305 if all controlling candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope and on at least one of the inserts in the required type size.
sections.conclusion: The requirements of section 84305 would be met if all controlling candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope, and, in addition, on at least one of the inserts in the required type size. Inclusion of candidates' names in the letterhead would satisfy the sender identification requirement for inserts. If controlling candidates are members of the measure committee's board of directors, their names may appear in a margin list of directors in requisite type size, with no special emphasis required so long as they are clearly identified as directors.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the sender identification requirements under Government Code section 84305 for mass mailings sent by a ballot measure committee controlled by multiple candidates, and how should the candidates' names appear on such mailings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirmed that sender identification requirements are satisfied when all controlling candidates are listed on the envelope exterior and on at least one insert in required type size, with flexibility in placement such as committee letterhead or director listings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains the original August 9, 1989 request letter from the attorney, an August 11, 1989 acknowledgment letter from FPPC, and the December 14, 1990 confirmation letter from General Counsel Kathryn Donovan. The final letter explicitly confirms the prior telephone advice. Some image quality issues in transmittal form but main advice sections are clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You had inquired about compliance with sender identification requirements for multiple-candidate controlled ballot measure committees. Specifically, regarding whether a multiple-candidate controlled ballot measure committee would be in compliance with Government Code section 84305 if all controlling candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope and on at least one of the inserts in the required type size.

CONCLUSION: The requirements of section 84305 would be met if all controlling candidates were listed on the outside of the envelope, and, in addition, on at least one of the inserts in the required type size. Inclusion of candidates' names in the letterhead would satisfy the sender identification requirement for inserts. If controlling candidates are members of the measure committee's board of directors, their names may appear in a margin list of directors in requisite type size, with no special emphasis required so long as they are clearly identified as directors.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter (dated December 14, 1990) confirming prior oral advice given in August 1989 to attorney Judith Davis regarding sender identification requirements for mass mailings by multi-candidate controlled ballot measure committees under Government Code section 84305.
